metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A rabbit-like beastfolk city where Rabbit Moon-Kitchen Warren of the Jade Mortar, Terrace Granary Beacon, Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Moonhollow should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Moonhollow organizes power through Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court, public response through Terrace Granary Beacon, civic memory through Rabbit Moon-Kitchen Warren of the Jade Mortar, and daily life through S1 Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, S2 Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Arena / Training Civic Hall, S5 Low Residential Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Chalkstone, Willow, Seashell, Limewash, Woven Reed, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Hearth Blessing, and exact anchors including Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court, Terrace Granary Beacon, Rabbit Moon-Kitchen Warren of the Jade Mortar, Tenement Court, Grain Hall, Training Arena, Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook, Granary / Food Market Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A rabbit-like beastfolk civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Rabbit Moon-Kitchen Warren of the Jade Mortar, Terrace Granary Beacon, Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court, and section anchors such as Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Civic Services Hall, Arena / Training Civic Hall, Low Residential Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Moonhollow as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.
